name: Cambrils to Castell d'Escolnarbou by gravel bike
name_original: De Cambrils al Castell d’Escolnarbou en bici de gravel
description:
This circular route from the Cambrils train station is ideal for a morning, or full day, of enjoyment, pedaling and gastronomy. The route escapes main roads and passes through charming towns such as Riudoms, Montbrió del Camp or Riudecanyes, each perfect for a gastronomic stop.

The sporting highlight is the ascent to Castell d'Escolnarbou, a medieval stronghold among the hills covered by the Mediterranean forest of pines and oaks. To link the modernity of the coast with the traditions of the castle, you pedal along a beautiful network of gravel and asphalt tracks that cross a tapestry of orchards of all kinds.

We recommend doing this outing on sunny days from autumn to spring. Winter in this area is very mild and pleasant to ride quietly. Although the Route is not very long, it is preferable that you bring some spare parts, tools and snacks to help you out of any unforeseen event.

To finish, you can make a reservation at one of the restaurants on the Cambrils promenade and treat yourself to a gastronomic treat before returning home. What better ending to this adventure!
region_intro: Gravel biking around Cambrils offers diverse landscapes, from coastal paths along the Mediterranean to challenging inland routes. The region features a network of rural roads, some paved and others traversing cultivated fields, extending towards the Llaberia Mountains. Cyclists can explore varied terrain, including significant elevation gains in the mountainous areas and flatter sections along the coast. The area provides access to natural features such as the Riudecanyes Reservoir and views of the Costa Daurada. Best gravel bike trails around Cambrils The most popular gravel bike trail is Cambrils to Castell d'Escolnarbou by gravel bike, a 37.1 miles (59.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 48 minutes to complete. This difficult route leads to the historic Castell d'Escolnarbou. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Montbrió del Camp – View of the Duesaigües Viaduct loop from Aparcament de pagament Cesar Augustus, a difficult 30.4 miles (49.0 km) path. This route offers views of the Duesaigües Viaduct. Local gravel bikers also love the Cambrils sign – Montbrió del Camp loop from Cambrils, a 31.4 miles (50.6 km) trail leading through rural roads and cultivated fields, often completed in about 3 hours 34 minutes. Gravel biking around Cambrils is defined by coastal paths, rural roads, and the Llaberia Mountains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easier coastal rides to more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Cambrils are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 15 reviews. More than 400 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Cambrils's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many gravel bike trails are available around Cambrils?
A: There are over 35 gravel bike trails available around Cambrils, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored these routes extensively, with more than 400 gravel bikers using komoot to discover the area's varied terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on gravel bike trails around Cambrils?
A: Gravel biking around Cambrils offers diverse landscapes. You can expect scenic coastal paths along the Mediterranean, challenging inland routes with significant elevation gains towards the Llaberia Mountains and Coll de la Teixeta, and a network of rural roads that traverse cultivated fields or follow dried-up stream beds. Some routes also pass by natural features like the Riudecanyes Reservoir.

Q: What is the best time of year for gravel biking in Cambrils?
A: Cambrils benefits from a pleasant Mediterranean climate, which allows for year-round outdoor enjoyment. Spring and autumn are particularly ideal for gravel biking, offering comfortable temperatures and less crowded trails. However, the mild winters also provide good conditions for cycling.

Q: Are there easy gravel bike routes suitable for beginners in Cambrils?
A: Yes, Cambrils offers options for beginners. The scenic 9-kilometer seafront promenade provides a flat and enjoyable ride with Mediterranean views, perfect for an easier introduction to gravel biking. While most routes tend to be moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy route identified in the area, and many coastal sections are less demanding.

Q: Are there challenging gravel bike routes for experienced riders in Cambrils?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, gravel routes extend towards the Llaberia Mountains and the Coll de la Teixeta mountain pass, offering significant elevation gains and panoramic views. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31047938" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de la Teixeta – Riudecanyes Reservoir loop from Cambrils&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 46.3 miles (74.5 km) with substantial climbing, are popular among advanced cyclists.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see along gravel bike trails in Cambrils?
A: Many gravel routes in Cambrils incorporate historical sites. You can cycle towards destinations like the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5137425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Miquel d'Escornalbou Castle and Monastery&lt;/a&gt;, or explore areas near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/345742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Escornalbou Castle-Monastery&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features other castles such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/226370" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola Castle, Colldejou&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4487615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell dels Teixells&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses into the area's rich history.

Q: Can I explore natural parks or scenic viewpoints on a gravel bike near Cambrils?
A: Yes, the region offers several natural attractions and viewpoints. You can find routes that lead to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31047938" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riudecanyes Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, offering serene views. The Llaberia Mountains provide rugged terrain and elevated viewpoints, and the mountaintop village of Siurana, though a bit further, offers spectacular views of its reservoir and surrounding areas. The Ebro Delta Natural Park, known for its beauty and birdwatching, is also within reach for a longer excursion.

Q: Are there any family-friendly gravel bike trails in Cambrils?
A: For families, the flat and enjoyable seafront promenade in Cambrils is an excellent option, providing a safe and scenic ride along the coast. While many inland gravel routes can be more challenging, the network of signposted rural roads within the Cambrils municipal district also includes easier sections that pass through cultivated fields, suitable for a more relaxed family outing.

Q: Are there circular gravel bike routes available around Cambrils?
A: Yes, many gravel bike routes around Cambrils are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31027336" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montbrió del Camp Town Hall – View of the Duesaigües Viaduct loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31047938" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de la Teixeta – Riudecanyes Reservoir loop&lt;/a&gt;, both offering diverse scenery and terrain.

Q: What do other gravel bikers enjoy most about gravel biking in Cambrils?
A: The gravel biking routes in Cambrils are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 15 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from the beautiful coastal paths to the challenging mountainous terrain, and the opportunity to explore charming rural roads and historical sites. The variety of routes caters to different abilities, making it appealing to a broad range of cyclists.

Q: Where can I find parking for gravel biking trails in Cambrils?
A: Cambrils offers various parking options. For some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31027336" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montbrió del Camp Town Hall – View of the Duesaigües Viaduct loop&lt;/a&gt;, specific starting points like Aparcament de pagament Cesar Augustus are noted. Generally, parking is available in and around the town, particularly near the port and main beaches, providing convenient access to coastal and inland routes.
tips: The cozy and authentic fishing village is known for the relaxed atmosphere that the life of fishermen brings with it. That quiet atmosphere is interrupted every working day around 17:00 by the fish auction in the harbor. Known in the far area for the high-quality fish that is sold here.
A beautiful and authentic town on the Costa Dorada, Riudoms is considered the birthplace of Antoni Gaudí, the famous architect of the Sagrada Familia in Barcelona.
It is a beautiful agricultural track surrounded by crop fields and with fantastic views of ancient towns and bucolic landscapes.
Magnificent paved track from Vilanova d'Escornalbou towards the castle of Escornalbou and the center of Argentera. Important slopes despite the short length and very quiet, no traffic.

The Castillo Monasterio de Escornalbou de Riudecanyes is a stately mansion from the beginning of the 20th century formed by the remains of two medieval buildings: the castle, built on the remains of a Roman fortress, and the monastery of Sant Miquel.
Good views of the Riudecanyes reservoir, used for irrigation and drinking water, from the water storage of the Riudecanyes stream, fed at the same time by the transfer of the Siurana river.

waypoints.name: Sant Miquel d'Escornalbou Castle and Monastery
